title: Exness Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Exness Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Exness (4.3★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Exness — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Exness?

Exness is a high-volume forex and CFD broker founded in 2008 and based in Limassol, Cyprus, handling over $3 trillion in monthly trading volume. The platform is built for traders who need tight spreads, fast execution, and instant withdrawal processing—earning it a 4.3/5 rating from active traders. All three account types (Standard, Raw Spread, and Zero) are completely free to open with no minimum deposit required, making it accessible to traders at any stage. Exness is best suited for high-volume forex scalpers, serious retail traders, and anyone frustrated with slow withdrawal timelines at traditional brokers.

How to Create Your Exness Account

Setting up an Exness account takes less than 10 minutes and requires minimal documentation to start trading immediately.

  • Step 1: Visit the signup page — Go to Exness.com and click "Open Account." You'll see a form asking for your email, password, and country of residence. Note that Exness is not available to residents of the United States or certain EU regions due to regulatory restrictions, so your location will be verified here.
  • Step 2: Provide basic personal information — Enter your full name, date of birth, phone number, and residential address. Exness will also ask about your trading experience level (beginner, intermediate, or advanced) to ensure appropriate account restrictions align with your knowledge.
  • Step 3: Choose your account type — You'll select between Standard (ideal for beginners with tighter spreads), Raw Spread (ECN-style with lower commissions), or Zero (raw spreads with micro-commissions). We'll cover the differences in detail below.
  • Step 4: Verification and approval — Upload a government-issued ID and proof of residence (utility bill or bank statement). Exness typically approves accounts within 24 hours. Once approved, you can fund your account and begin trading immediately.
  • Step 5: Fund your account — Exness accepts bank transfers, credit/debit cards, and e-wallets. Because there's no minimum deposit, you can start with as little as you're comfortable risking.

Total estimated setup time: 10-15 minutes for account opening, plus 24 hours for verification approval.

Setting Up Exness for the First Time

Once your account is approved and funded, familiarize yourself with the platform layout before placing your first trade.

Dashboard navigation: Log into your Exness account to access the main dashboard, which displays your account balance, equity, margin usage, and open positions at a glance. The left sidebar contains links to the terminal (trading interface), account settings, deposit/withdrawal options, and educational resources. Unlike many brokers that force proprietary platforms, Exness integrates with both MetaTrader 4 (MT4) and MetaTrader 5 (MT5)—industry-standard platforms that most traders already know.

Key settings to configure immediately: In your account settings, enable two-factor authentication (2FA) for security. Set your default leverage (Exness allows unlimited leverage, but beginners should cap this at 1:100 or 1:50 to avoid catastrophic losses). Configure your preferred base currency for P&L calculations, and turn on email/SMS alerts for margin warnings and significant price movements in your watchlist.

Connecting MT4 or MT5: Download MetaTrader from the Exness website (broker-specific builds ensure faster order execution). Log in using your Exness account credentials, and the platform will automatically connect to Exness's servers. From here, you can access all available instruments, set up custom indicators, and configure your trading layouts. Most advanced traders prefer MT5 for its superior charting tools and algorithmic trading capabilities, while MT4 remains the default for scalpers and high-frequency traders.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Paper Trading (Demo Account) — Before risking real money, activate Exness's paper trading feature through your account dashboard. You'll get a virtual $100,000 to practice your strategy for up to 90 days. This is essential for testing your risk management rules and becoming comfortable with the platform without financial pressure. Many new traders skip this step and regret it; use your demo account until you can consistently hit your targets.

2. Mobile App and Web Trader — Exness offers both native iOS/Android apps and a browser-based Web Trader, allowing you to monitor positions and execute trades from anywhere. The mobile app includes price alerts and one-click position closure, critical for scalpers and swing traders who can't be glued to their desk. The Web Trader requires no download and works on any device with a browser.

3. API Access for Automated Trading — If you're building trading bots or connecting to third-party algorithmic trading systems, Exness provides full REST and WebSocket API access at no additional cost. Your API key can be generated directly from your account dashboard, and the documentation is clear enough for developers to integrate within hours.

4. Risk Management Tools and Alerts — Configure stop-loss orders, take-profit levels, and margin call alerts to automate your risk controls. Exness sends real-time SMS and email notifications when your account approaches margin limits, preventing forced liquidation from surprise slippage. You can also set price alerts on specific instruments to notify you when critical support or resistance levels are breached.

5. Custom Indicators and Expert Advisors — Both MT4 and MT5 allow you to import custom indicators and automated trading algorithms. Exness supports all standard MQL4 and MQL5 code, meaning you can run strategies from freelance platforms like MQL5 Marketplace or build your own. This is where Exness shines for technical traders who want full customization beyond basic charting.

6. Social and Broker Integration Features — Exness integrates with signal copying and social trading platforms, allowing you to follow professional traders' positions or share your own trading activity. This feature is useful for learning trading psychology by shadowing experienced traders, though you should never blindly copy without understanding the strategy.

7. Educational Resources and Webinars — Exness provides free trading education through video tutorials, written guides, and live webinars. The resources cover everything from forex basics to advanced hedging strategies, and they're available 24/7 in multiple languages.

Exness Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

All three Exness account types are completely free to open—there are no monthly fees, account maintenance charges, or hidden costs. Your only expenses are the spreads you pay per trade and any commissions on certain account types.

Standard Account ($0/mo): The Standard account targets beginners and intermediate traders. Spreads start at 0.1 pips on major currency pairs (EUR/USD, GBP/USD), making it tighter than most retail brokers but slightly wider than ECN alternatives. There are no commissions—you only pay the spread. Standard accounts allow up to 1:unlimited leverage, though you can voluntarily reduce this in your settings.

Raw Spread Account ($0/mo): The Raw Spread account is designed for active traders and scalpers who want the tightest possible spreads without per-trade commissions. Spreads here can be as low as 0.01 pips on EUR/USD during peak hours (London/US open), with a $10 per 1 million traded. This means if you're scalping high volumes, Raw Spread is significantly cheaper than Standard.

Zero Account ($0/mo): The Zero account offers fixed zero spreads on majors (the broker instead charges a small per-trade commission, typically $1-3 per standard lot). This is ideal for scalpers who are comfortable paying tiny commissions in exchange for guaranteed, predictable entry prices with zero slippage on the spread component.

Recommendation by trader type: Beginners should start with Standard—the 0.1 pip spread is tight enough that you won't bleed money on spreads, and there's no commission to confuse your P&L calculations. Intermediate swing traders should also stick with Standard unless you're trading more than 50 lots per week. If you're scalping or day trading high volumes (100+ lots daily), switch to Raw Spread or Zero for cost savings that will quickly compound. Traders want to compare spreads at Exness with competitors—see how it stacks up in our Exness vs OANDA comparison and Exness vs IG Markets review.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Exness

  • Use unlimited leverage intentionally, not by default. Exness allows unlimited leverage, but this is a footgun for most traders. Immediately reduce your account leverage to 1:50 or 1:100 in your settings. Unlimited leverage is tempting when you're down, but it's designed for professional money managers with risk protocols, not retail traders.
  • Test the instant withdrawal feature early. One of Exness's best competitive advantages is instant withdrawal processing. On your first withdrawal, request a small amount to confirm it arrives in your bank account or e-wallet within minutes. This removes friction if you ever need to exit the platform quickly due to a rule change or personal emergency.
  • Schedule your trading around the London/US open overlap. Spreads on Exness are tightest during the London (8am-12pm GMT) and US (1pm-5pm GMT) overlap when liquidity peaks. If you're a scalper on Raw Spread or Zero accounts, your $3T+ monthly volume advantage is most pronounced during these 4 hours. Avoid trading during Asian hours unless you're specifically trading Asian pairs.
  • Use the API to automate order entry and risk management. If you use trading bots or algorithmic strategies, Exness's API is free and low-latency. Automating stop-losses and position sizing eliminates emotion and ensures you never miss a trade setup due to screen fatigue. Even a simple bot that sizes positions based on your daily risk limit will improve your consistency.
  • Enable price alerts instead of staring at charts all day. Exness's alert system is underutilized by retail traders. Instead of watching 5 charts for hours, set price alerts on key support/resistance levels and focus on higher-impact activities. When the alert fires, you have 30 seconds to assess the context and execute. This reduces trader burnout and improves decision quality.
  • Leverage the paper trading account to practice new strategies quarterly. Spend 1-2 weeks on the demo account testing any new strategy, indicator, or position sizing rule before deploying it with real money. Your demo account resets every 90 days—use this as a natural opportunity to audit your trading journal and identify what's working.
  • Monitor the $3T+ monthly volume as a proxy for liquidity. Exness's massive volume means order fills are almost never rejected, and slippage is minimal even during volatile news events. If you're considering switching from a smaller broker, test your largest position sizes on a demo account first to confirm fills match your expectations.

Common Exness Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "I'm not approved because I'm in a restricted region." — Exness cannot serve US residents, and some EU countries have blocked the platform due to regulatory pressures. If you're in a restricted region, there are no workarounds—Exness is legally obligated to decline your application. Consider alternatives like OANDA (US-approved) or IG Markets (EU-regulated).

Issue 2: "My withdrawal is showing as pending." — Exness advertises instant withdrawals, but occasionally payment processors queue requests during high-volume periods (weekends or news events). Check your email for any action items from the payment processor (some require SMS verification). If it's been more than 2 hours, contact Exness support via live chat—they usually resolve within 30 minutes.

Issue 3: "MetaTrader disconnected and I can't reconnect." — Close MetaTrader completely, check that Exness servers are online (visit status.exness.com), then restart MT4/MT5. Verify your password hasn't changed and that 2FA codes are synced correctly if enabled. If the issue persists, try the Web Trader as a backup—Exness's web platform is often more stable than desktop clients.

Issue 4: "My margin call came without warning during low liquidity." — This happens when account leverage is too high relative to your position sizes. After the margin call, immediately reduce your leverage setting and recalculate your position sizes using a proper position sizing formula (e.g., 2% account risk per trade). Exness's alerts should have warned you; verify SMS and email alerts are actually enabled in your account settings.

Is Exness Worth It? Our Verdict

Exness is genuinely worth using if you're a high-volume forex trader or scalper outside the US/EU and prioritize tight spreads and instant withdrawals over brand recognition. The zero account minimum, free tier (demo), and integration with MetaTrader standards make it accessible and familiar. However, if you're a beginner with small account sizes, the spread advantage evaporates—Standard spreads at Exness (0.1 pips) are only marginally better than retail competitors, so beginners might prefer platforms with better education or mobile-first designs. The unlimited leverage is a con, not a pro, for 90% of retail traders. Bottom line: use Exness for its technical strengths (API, tight spreads on Raw/Zero, instant withdrawals, $3T volume), not because it's cheaper than OANDA or IG—because on small account sizes, it isn't.

Ready to open an account? See our full Exness review for detailed performance data and user feedback.
